article_title: Google Expands Universal Commerce Protocol With Cart, Catalog, and Identity Linking as Agentic Shopping Takes Shape

content_preview.title: Google Expands Universal Commerce Protocol With Cart, Catalog, and Identity Linking as Agentic Shopping Takes Shape
content_preview.summary: Google added Cart, Catalog, and Identity Linking capabilities to its Universal Commerce Protocol, expanding the open standard that lets AI agents complete purchases across retailer platforms.
content_preview.body_excerpt: Google on March 19 released three new optional capabilities for the Universal Commerce Protocol, the open standard for AI-driven shopping that the company launched in January alongside Shopify, Target, Walmart, and more than 20 other retailers and payment processors. The additions -- Cart, Catalog, and Identity Linking -- mark the protocol's first major functional expansion and bring it closer to enabling AI agents to handle multi-step purchase workflows [without human intervention](https://blog...

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Well-sourced commerce tech news. Ready for publication.
